records.ety: [OF. constraindre, F. contrainde, L. constringere; con- + stringere to draw tight. See Strain, and. cf. Constrict, Constringe.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Constrained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Constraining.]

defs: To secure by bonds; to chain; to bond or confine; to hold tightly; to constringe.
quotes: He binds in chains The drowsy prophet, and his limbs constrains.
When winter frosts constrain the fields with cold.

defs: To bring into a narrow compass; to compress.
quotes: How the strait stays the slender waist constrain.

defs: To hold back by force; to restrain; to repress.
quotes: My sire in caves constrains the winds.

defs: To compel; to force; to necessitate; to oblige.
quotes: The love of Christ constraineth us.
I was constrained to appeal unto Cæsar.

defs: To produce in such a manner as to give an unnatural effect; as, a constrained voice.
